title: Budget vs Actuals — Monthly Variance Analysis
description: Finance analysts comparing October 2024 actual spend against budget across departments and accounts. Models a real FP&A month-end close workflow.

sheets.name: Actuals
sheets.role: Raw ERP journal export — source of October actual amounts

columns.name: Department
columns.note: One row intentionally has a trailing space — causes a silent SUMIFS miss, learner should note it but not fix it

columns.name: Account
columns.note: Account 6100-01 only exists as sub-accounts 6100-01-A and 6100-01-B in this sheet — no parent row

columns.name: Amount
columns.format: Number — Revenue accounts (4xxx) are NEGATIVE, Expense accounts are POSITIVE

columns.name: DupFlag
columns.role: Learner fills T01 COUNTIFS formula here

data_characteristics: 53 journal rows — data in rows 3-55, header at row 2, note banner at row 1
Multiple rows per dept/account combination (e.g. two rows for DEPT-101/6100-01-A and DEPT-101/6100-01-B)
Two JournalIDs each appear exactly twice (duplicates for T01)
Revenue accounts (4xxx) stored as negative — budget stores them as positive (planning convention)

sheets.name: Budget
sheets.role: Planned budget amounts by department and account code for October 2024

columns.name: DeptCode
columns.format: D-prefix (e.g. D-SALES, D-MKTG, D-ENG, D-CS, D-GNA, D-OPS, D-PROD, D-HR, D-LEG, D-FIN)

columns.name: AccountCode
columns.format: NNNN-NN parent codes only (e.g. 6100-01) — never sub-accounts like 6100-01-A

columns.name: BudgetAmount
columns.format: Always a positive number for both Revenue and Expense (planning convention — opposite of Actuals sign)

data_characteristics: 42 budget rows — data in rows 3-44, header at row 2, note at row 1
No zero BudgetAmount values exist — a SUMIFS result of 0 always means no matching row was found
5 dept+account combinations in Variance_Workings have NO matching row in Budget (these should return 'Not Budgeted')
3 discontinued accounts exist in Budget with non-zero amounts but have zero Actuals

sheets.name: DeptMap
sheets.role: Cross-reference mapping ERP dept codes to Budget dept codes
data_characteristics: One ERPCode row has a trailing space — TRIM() required in XLOOKUP to avoid silent miss

sheets.name: AccountMap
sheets.role: Chart of accounts — maps account codes to names and types

sheets.name: Variance_Workings
sheets.role: Main analysis sheet — learner progressively fills columns B, C, E, F, I through M

columns.name: Actual
columns.format: Number — pre-filled with October actuals (aggregated from Actuals sheet)

columns.name: Budget
columns.format: Positive number or text 'Not Budgeted' — learner fills (T04)

columns.name: PctVariance
columns.format: Decimal ratio or text 'N/A' — learner fills (T06)

data_characteristics: 47 data rows — rows 2-48, header at row 1
Rows 41-45 are accounts with no matching Budget row — col I should show 'Not Budgeted' for these
Rows 46-48 are discontinued accounts — non-zero budget, zero actuals
Col H (Actual) is pre-populated — learner does NOT fill this column

tasks.title: Spot the Duplicate Journal Entry
tasks.controllerAsk: In H3:H55 on the Actuals sheet, enter a COUNTIFS formula that counts how many times each JournalID (column A) appears in A3:A55. Cells with count > 1 are duplicates.
tasks.tutorFocus: =COUNTIFS($A$3:$A$55,A3). Lock the range with $. Any cell showing 2 is a duplicate. Copy down H3:H55.

tasks.title: Map ERP Dept Codes to Budget Codes
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill B2:B48 on Variance_Workings with an XLOOKUP (or VLOOKUP) that maps each ERP dept code (col A) to its Budget dept code using the DeptMap tab. Use TRIM() to handle potential trailing spaces.
tasks.tutorFocus: =XLOOKUP(TRIM(A2),DeptMap!A:A,DeptMap!B:B,"#N/A"). TRIM protects against the trailing-space trap on row 16. Copy down to B48.

tasks.title: Look Up Account Names
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill E2:E48 with an XLOOKUP that returns the Account_Name from AccountMap for each account code in column D.

tasks.title: Pull Budget Amounts
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill I2:I48 with a formula that looks up the budget amount from the Budget tab matching dept code (col B) AND account code (col D). Return 'Not Budgeted' with IFERROR if no match exists.

tasks.title: Calculate Dollar Variance
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill J2:J48 with: IFERROR(H2-I2, H2). This handles 'Not Budgeted' rows — IFERROR catches the text-subtraction error and returns H2.
tasks.tutorFocus: =IFERROR(H2-I2,H2). If I2 is 'Not Budgeted' (text), H2-I2 errors — IFERROR returns H2. Copy down to J48.

tasks.title: Calculate % Variance
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill K2:K48 with: IFERROR(J2/I2, "N/A"). Rows with 'Not Budgeted' in col I will return 'N/A'.
tasks.tutorFocus: =IFERROR(J2/I2,"N/A"). Division by text or zero errors — returns 'N/A'. Format col K as percentage. Copy down to K48.

tasks.title: Flag Material Variances
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill L2:L48 with: IF(AND(ABS(J2)>=25000, ISNUMBER(K2), ABS(K2)>=0.1), "Yes", "No").
tasks.tutorFocus: =IF(AND(ABS(J2)>=25000,ISNUMBER(K2),ABS(K2)>=0.1),"Yes","No"). ISNUMBER(K2) filters out 'N/A' rows. Copy down to L48.

tasks.title: Classify Favorable / Unfavorable
tasks.controllerAsk: Fill M2:M48 with: IF(L2<>"Yes","", IF(F2="Revenue", IF(ABS(H2)>I2,"F","U"), IF(H2<I2,"F","U"))). Col F holds AccountType.
tasks.tutorFocus: =IF(L2<>"Yes","",IF(F2="Revenue",IF(ABS(H2)>I2,"F","U"),IF(H2<I2,"F","U"))). Revenue: ABS(actuals) vs budget — ERP stores revenue negative. Expenses: direct comparison. Copy to M48.
